_comment: This file tracks intentional divergences from stock JHipster. It is loaded by the /jhipster-upgrade skill and should be updated after each upgrade. Place a project-specific copy at .jhipster-divergences.json in the project root if you want per-project overrides.

_schema.area: build | database | config | security | tooling | dependencies

divergence: No Jib plugin for Docker builds
action: Skip jib-maven-plugin in pom.xml plugins and pluginManagement

reason: Project uses root docker-compose.yml and/or custom dev folder
action: Skip all references to src/main/docker/ in config and scripts
Skip frontend-maven-plugin and node/npm related config

reason: Business requirement (e.g., MSSQL, Oracle instead of PostgreSQL)
action: Keep project's DB driver, liquibase extensions, JDBC URLs, and test containers. Skip reference DB driver and test containers
Remove spring.docker.compose section if copied from reference. Skip docker-compose profile
MERGE new JHipster fields into existing class, never overwrite

reason: Production resilience settings (e.g., TCP keepalive for cloud Redis)
action: After copying reference CacheConfiguration, restore custom client bean, tuning, and entity cache entries

reason: Business endpoints with specific auth rules
action: After copying reference SecurityConfiguration, restore all custom requestMatchers

reason: Project uses CI/CD-provided tooling instead of npm

divergence: Project-specific dependencies not in stock JHipster
reason: Business requirements (e.g., cloud SDKs, PDF libs, custom drivers)
action: Keep all project-specific deps. Never remove them during upgrade
